task:
ReadCheckpointMetadata loads into memory a metadata json file and then unmarshal it. It goes on to then do the same for its sessions, which can be many and large files. Overall this approach has a huge impact on memory churn.

A lot of its calls come from tests which justify the need to go into so much detail (and potentially have it all in memory). However, production code seems to only need a tiny proportion of that data.

Based on the above:
1. Map all the uses for ReadCheckpointMetadata.
2. Propose optimisations whereby data is either only loaded on demand, or only a subset of the data is pre-loaded.

The existing code moves to a _test.go, so that it is still available for the test code. However, a new version is created that solely loads into the memory the information needed - also not doing a full read into memory and unmarshal, but instead scanning through the input stream (json decoder).

Ensure that all sessions are processed.

Don't ask questions and just go ahead with the changes.
